questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I am happy," she said.
options: She said she is happy.
She said she was happy.
She said she has been happy.
She said she had been happy.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She said she was happy.' In reported speech, when the reporting verb is in the past (said), the present tense changes to the past tense. 'I am' becomes 'she was'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I visited my grandmother yesterday," he said.
options: He said he visited his grandmother yesterday.
He said he had visited his grandmother yesterday.
He said he visited his grandmother the day before.
He said he had visited his grandmother the day before.
explanation: The correct answer is 'He said he had visited his grandmother the day before.' In reported speech, Past Simple changes to Past Perfect ('visited' → 'had visited'), and 'yesterday' changes to 'the day before'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I will help you," she promised.
options: She promised she will help me.
She promised she would help me.
She promised she helps me.
She promised she helped me.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She promised she would help me.' 'Will' changes to 'would' in reported speech. Also, 'you' changes to 'me' because the speaker is reporting what was said to them.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I have finished my homework," the student said.
options: The student said he finished his homework.
The student said he has finished his homework.
The student said he had finished his homework.
The student said he was finishing his homework.
explanation: The correct answer is 'The student said he had finished his homework.' Present Perfect ('have finished') changes to Past Perfect ('had finished') in reported speech. Also, 'my' changes to 'his' to match the subject.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I was reading when you called," she told me.
options: She told me she was reading when I called.
She told me she had been reading when I called.
She told me she is reading when I called.
She told me she has been reading when I called.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She told me she had been reading when I called.' Past Continuous ('was reading') changes to Past Perfect Continuous ('had been reading') in reported speech. Also, 'you' changes to 'I' because the speaker is the person being addressed.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I can speak three languages," he boasted.
options: He boasted he can speak three languages.
He boasted he could speak three languages.
He boasted he has been able to speak three languages.
He boasted he is able to speak three languages.
explanation: The correct answer is 'He boasted he could speak three languages.' The modal 'can' changes to 'could' in reported speech when the reporting verb is in the past.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Where do you live?" she asked me.
options: She asked me where do I live.
She asked me where I live.
She asked me where did I live.
She asked me where I lived.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She asked me where I lived.' In reported questions, the word order changes to subject + verb (no inversion), and the tense changes: Present Simple ('do you live') becomes Past Simple ('I lived').

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Are you coming to the party?" they asked.
options: They asked if I was coming to the party.
They asked if I am coming to the party.
They asked if was I coming to the party.
They asked if I come to the party.
explanation: The correct answer is 'They asked if I was coming to the party.' For yes/no questions, we use 'if' or 'whether', and the word order changes to subject + verb. 'Are you coming' becomes 'I was coming' (tense backshift and pronoun change).

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "What time does the train leave?" he inquired.
options: He inquired what time does the train leave.
He inquired what time the train leaves.
He inquired what time the train left.
He inquired what time did the train leave.
explanation: The correct answer is 'He inquired what time the train left.' In reported wh-questions, the wh-word remains, the word order becomes subject + verb (no inversion), and the tense changes: 'does the train leave' becomes 'the train left' (Past Simple).

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I will be here tomorrow," she said.
options: She said she would be here tomorrow.
She said she would be there tomorrow.
She said she would be here the next day.
She said she would be there the next day.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She said she would be there the next day.' In reported speech, 'will' changes to 'would', 'here' changes to 'there', and 'tomorrow' changes to 'the next day'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Don't touch this!" the teacher warned.
options: The teacher warned to not touch this.
The teacher warned me not to touch that.
The teacher warned me don't touch that.
The teacher warned I didn't touch that.
explanation: The correct answer is 'The teacher warned me not to touch that.' To report an order or warning, we use 'tell/order/warn + object + (not) + to-infinitive'. Also, 'this' changes to 'that'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Please close the door," she asked.
options: She asked to close the door.
She asked me close the door.
She asked me to close the door.
She asked me if I close the door.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She asked me to close the door.' To report a request, we use 'ask + object + to-infinitive'. The word 'please' is omitted in reported speech.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I went to Douala last week," he said.
options: He said he went to Douala last week.
He said he had gone to Douala last week.
He said he had gone to Douala the previous week.
He said he has gone to Douala the previous week.
explanation: The correct answer is 'He said he had gone to Douala the previous week.' Past Simple ('went') changes to Past Perfect ('had gone'), and 'last week' changes to 'the previous week'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I must finish this report today," she said.
options: She said she must finish this report today.
She said she had to finish that report that day.
She said she must finish that report that day.
She said she had to finish this report today.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She said she had to finish that report that day.' 'Must' can change to 'had to' in reported speech. Also, 'this' changes to 'that' and 'today' changes to 'that day'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Have you ever visited Cameroon?" the tourist asked me.
options: The tourist asked me if I have ever visited Cameroon.
The tourist asked me if I had ever visited Cameroon.
The tourist asked me if I ever visited Cameroon.
The tourist asked me if did I ever visit Cameroon.
explanation: The correct answer is 'The tourist asked me if I had ever visited Cameroon.' For yes/no questions, we use 'if' or 'whether'. Present Perfect ('Have you ever visited') changes to Past Perfect ('I had ever visited').

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I saw the accident," the witness said.
options: The witness said he saw the accident.
The witness said he has seen the accident.
The witness said he had seen the accident.
The witness said he was seeing the accident.
explanation: The correct answer is 'The witness said he had seen the accident.' Past Simple ('saw') changes to Past Perfect ('had seen') in reported speech. Also, 'I' changes to 'he' to match the witness.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Why didn't you come?" he asked me.
options: He asked me why didn't I come.
He asked me why I didn't come.
He asked me why I hadn't come.
He asked me why I hadn't come.
explanation: The correct answer is 'He asked me why I hadn't come.' In reported wh-questions, the word order becomes subject + verb (no inversion). Past Simple negative ('didn't come') changes to Past Perfect negative ('hadn't come').

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I love this music," she whispered.
options: She whispered she loves that music.
She whispered she loved this music.
She whispered she loved that music.
She whispered she has loved that music.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She whispered she loved that music.' Present Simple ('love') changes to Past Simple ('loved'), and 'this' changes to 'that'.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Please help me," the child cried.
options: The child cried to help me.
The child cried me to help.
The child cried to help him.
The child cried me to help him.
explanation: The correct answer is 'The child cried to help him.' To report a request, we use 'ask/beg/cry + to-infinitive'. The pronoun 'me' changes to 'him' because the child is being reported.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "I was born in Bafoussam," he told us.
options: He told us he was born in Bafoussam.
He told us he has been born in Bafoussam.
He told us he had been born in Bafoussam.
He told us he is born in Bafoussam.
explanation: The correct answer is 'He told us he was born in Bafoussam.' When the verb in direct speech is already in the past (was born), it remains in the past in reported speech. This is an exception to the usual backshift rule because the statement is a permanent fact.

questionText: Transform into reported speech: "Who is that man?" she wondered.
options: She wondered who is that man.
She wondered who that man is.
She wondered who that man was.
She wondered who was that man.
explanation: The correct answer is 'She wondered who that man was.' In reported wh-questions, the wh-word remains, the word order becomes subject + verb (no inversion), 'is' changes to 'was', and 'that' remains 'that' (or can change to 'that' if appropriate).
